prompt.role: Senior Python Developer (LLM / AI Chatbot Engineer)
prompt.task:
Implement pseudo-memory for a Python AI chat bot by storing per-user conversation history and sending the full history to the model on each new request.
Goal Add support for message history so that the bot can preserve context across multiple messages from the same user.
Functional requirements 1. Implement storage of conversation history between the user and the bot. 2. Store messages in the following format: [ { "role": "user", "content": "..." }, { "role": "assistant", "content": "..." } ] 3. On each new user request: - load the existing history for that specific user; - append the new user message; - send the full history plus the new message to the model; - save the assistant response back into that same user’s history. 4. Conversation history must be stored separately for each user. 5. The implementation must not mix dialogs of different users. 6. The current bot behavior should remain intact except for the added memory feature.
Expected behavior Example for one user: - User sends: "Hi" - History sent to model: [ { "role": "user", "content": "Hi" } ] - Assistant replies: "Hello!" - Stored history becomes: [ { "role": "user", "content": "Hi" }, { "role": "assistant", "content": "Hello!" } ] - Then user sends: "What did I just say?" - The model should receive: [ { "role": "user", "content": "Hi" }, { "role": "assistant", "content": "Hello!" }, { "role": "user", "content": "What did I just say?" } ]
Technical requirements 1. Identify users by a stable unique user identifier already available in the bot. 2. Create a storage mechanism for history per user. 3. Prefer a simple and clear implementation first: - in-memory dictionary for prototype, or - file-based JSON storage, or - SQLite if the project already uses persistent storage. 4. Encapsulate history operations into separate functions or a dedicated service, for example: - get_history(user_id) - append_message(user_id, role, content) - save_history(user_id, history) - clear_history(user_id) if useful 5. Before calling the model: - retrieve the user’s existing history; - append the current user message to the request payload. 6. After receiving the model response: - append the assistant response to the same user history.
Non-functional requirements 1. Keep the code clean, modular, and easy to extend later. 2. Do not hardcode logic for only one user. 3. Avoid duplicated code. 4. Handle empty history correctly. 5. Add basic error handling for corrupted or missing history data. 6. Do not introduce breaking changes into the current bot flow.
Deliverables 1. Updated Python bot code with pseudo-memory support. 2. Clear separation of history by user. 3. Refactored logic so that history handling is isolated in a dedicated module/service. 4. Short documentation or comments explaining: - where history is stored, - how it is retrieved, - how it is sent to the model.
Acceptance criteria The task is complete only if all of the following are true: 1. Messages are stored in the format: { "role": "...", "content": "..." } 2. Each user has an independent conversation history. 3. On every new request, the model receives: - previous history for that user, - plus the new user message. 4. After the response, the assistant message is saved in the same history. 5. Two different users do not share context. 6. The bot still works correctly after the change.
